steps.title: Use the ink date
steps.content: The team found titanium dioxide in the Vinland Map's ink. Because titanium dioxide was first introduced in ink manufacturing in the early 1900s, a map containing it could not have been produced by mapmakers in the mid-1400s.

steps.title: Keep the conclusion narrow
steps.content: A overreaches from ink chemistry to what Europeans could have known. C contradicts the timeline for titanium compounds in ink. D ignores that the post-1900 compound gives evidence about when the ink could have been made. The answer is B.
